D7 (runtime ApplyPlan schema gap) PARTIALLY RESOLVED. docs/schemas/apply-plan-runtime.schema.json (JSON Schema 2020-12, additionalProperties:false) locks the W3 Day-1b SwDocShell::applyDiagnosticsPlan() input envelope: schema_version const v2-w3-runtime-1, plan_id (^ap-...$), source_diagnostic_id (^diag-...$), doc_snapshot_hash (^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$), patches array (1-256 items, each with patch_id ^p[0-9]+$, kind enum 7-token, target.paragraph_id ^swpara-[0-9]+$, optional severity/rationale), preview_only bool. Per-kind internal shape (paragraph-replace before/after, paragraph-format format_changes, text-format range/format_changes, etc.) intentionally NOT locked at envelope level — each SwUndoApplyPatch impl (W3 Day-1b/c/d) lands per-kind validation alongside its production code. simplicity-first: avoid over-locking before patch impls exist. + 2 fixtures (apply-plan-runtime.{valid,invalid}.json) demonstrating 3 patch kinds (paragraph-replace + paragraph-format + text-format) and 5 distinct invalidations (BAD plan_id, non-sha256 hash, P_INVALID patch_id, bogus kind, severity 'critical' not in enum, paragraph_id missing 'swpara-' prefix). Distinct from V1.5 docs/schemas/apply-plan.schema.json (m3-02 capability descriptor: id/preview_action_id/capability_id/undo_group/failure_behavior — descriptor lives at capability registration time, not per-call). Added docs/schemas/fixtures/async-task.terminal-failed.json — extended-naming boundary fixture for the W5 async-task schema covering the terminal-failed branch of the state machine. Why this matters: async-task.valid.json covers the awaiting-review happy path (3 completed steps, real result_plan_id, full evidence_ids) and async-task.invalid.json blasts 11 schema violations at once, but neither exercises the *valid-but-terminal* path that the failure_reason field exists for. terminal-failed.json fills that gap: state='failed' + failure_reason populated (Provider call timeout at step s2 after 30s) + steps array carries one completed/one failed/one stuck-pending step (the canonical terminal-step rollup pattern) + result_plan_id=null (because the task never reaches awaiting-review when it dies before assembly) + evidence_ids reflects only the steps that produced evidence. Schema-level guarantees this fixture locks: state enum still accepts 'failed', failure_reason maxLength holds at 500 chars, step state enum [pending, running, completed, failed] (narrower than TaskState — no awaiting-review on steps) accepts mixed terminal states, result_plan_id oneOf null|^ap-[0-9a-f]{16}$ accepts null, evidence array can be a strict subset of step-evidence (the dead step still mints an evidence id even though it failed). Resolved as expectation=valid by bin/intelligent-contract-fixtures.sh extended-naming branch (basename first dot-segment 'async-task' → async-task.schema.json exists → expectation=valid). H2 baseline auto-fired the moment the fixture landed: 'Fixtures checked: 28' lock failed loudly, proving the regression detection works. Bumped to 29 fixtures across 12 schemas (schema count unchanged — no new schema, just one more fixture against the existing async-task schema). lane-status.md mirror: Wave roll-up W5 row updated 2 fixtures → 3 fixtures (incl. terminal-failed boundary), baseline 28/12 → 29/12; W5 detail block expanded to mention the terminal-failed fixture and the specific schema branches it locks; Authoritative artifacts ledger entry-count refreshed 42 → 43 (the H2 ledger-count check 9 catches this). 4-harness sweep verification: H1 26 checks passed; H2 26 checks 29 fixtures 12 schemas passed; H3 3-way drift lock passed; H4 Status: passed (partial) Mode: partial-enforce — H4 is asserting schema enum order + required-keys + 2 fixtures' own validity (it doesn't iterate every fixture, that's H2's job). ai-native cppunit suite total unchanged at 84. Added docs/schemas/fixtures/apply-plan-runtime.utf8.json — extended-naming UTF-8 multi-codepoint boundary fixture for the W3 apply-plan-runtime schema. Why this matters: W3 Day-1g already locked 5 cppunit cases for ApplyPlanValidator's UTF-8 length counting (4-byte emoji, fullwidth punctuation, escaped quotes, BOM, embedded NUL guard) — the fixture half of the lock was missing, so future schema-only edits could pass without ever seeing a real multi-codepoint payload. apply-plan-runtime.utf8.json fills that gap with 5 patches each exercising a distinct UTF-8 boundary class: p1 single 4-byte 🎉 (U+1F389) + fullwidth Chinese punctuation (validates byte-length count not screen-column count), p2 ZWJ family emoji 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 11-byte 1-grapheme sequence (validates range.length walks bytes not grapheme clusters — a real risk because UNO range types historically wobble on this), p3 nested escaped quotes 'foo "bar" baz' (validates apv_readString primitive doesn't terminate on the escaped quote), p4 RTL mix Hebrew שָׁלוֹם + Arabic السَّلَام + CJK (validates BiDi reading-order is left to vcl + the validator only does byte-level checks, no semantic transcoding), p5 CJK extension B 𠀾𠁀𠁁 (U+20000+, supplementary plane 4-byte UTF-8, exercises the boundary where naïve UTF-16 surrogate-pair handling would split a codepoint). All 5 patches are schema-valid (preview_only:true so this can land before W3 Day-1b SwDocShell wiring lands). Resolved as expectation=valid by bin/intelligent-contract-fixtures.sh extended-naming branch (basename first dot-segment 'apply-plan-runtime' → apply-plan-runtime.schema.json exists → expectation=valid). Added docs/schemas/async-task.schema.md — 202-line reader's guide for the W5 async-task envelope. Why this matters: the schema body itself (async-task.schema.json) is the single source of truth, but JSON Schema 2020-12 syntax doesn't carry the *why* — why is service_mode locked at creation, why is the step state enum narrower than TaskState, why is failure_reason capped at 500 chars. A downstream tool author or the W5 Day-0 C++ implementer hand-deriving AsyncTask.hxx would have to either re-derive every invariant from the spec + schema cross-walk or risk silently violating one. The reader's manual fills that gap with 10 sections: §1 file location + scope (one JSON file per task under ai-tasks/YYYY-MM/, distinct from provider-evidence and apply-plan-runtime), §2 11-required-key table with per-key 'why required' column citing the spec/incident that locks it, §3 step object internals + why step.state is narrower than TaskState (apply-plan rollup is task-level not step-level), §4 3 optional keys with their state-conditional invariants (failure_reason required iff state=failed, result_plan_id null while running, review_decisions populated only at awaiting-review), §5 'What's NOT locked' deferred-shape list (per-kind input shape, per-step output shape, cron schedule, companion-app pairing, sub-second timestamps) — these are the boundaries that a future reader needs to know are *intentionally* open, §6 ASCII state-machine diagram with no-re-entry invariant + updated_at invariant + result_plan_id invariant, §7 fixture coverage matrix (3 fixtures: valid happy-path / invalid 11-violations / terminal-failed boundary L43), §8 5-step reader checklist for downstream tools (verify schema_version, validate additionalProperties:false walk, cross-check state↔result_plan_id invariant, cross-check state↔failure_reason invariant, de-dup evidence_ids vs steps[].evidence), §9 authority + change control (any schema change requires same-batch updates to spec/lane-status/baseline test/this doc), §10 cross-reference list. lane-status.md mirror: §'Schemas (V2)' async-task entry now points at the reader's manual (L45); Authoritative artifacts ledger entry-count refreshed 44 → 45 (caught by H2 ledger-count check 9). 4-harness sweep verification: H1 26 checks passed; H2 26 checks 30 fixtures 12 schemas passed; H3 3-way drift lock passed; H4 Status: passed (partial) Mode: partial-enforce. ai-native cppunit suite total unchanged at 84. V1.5 27/27 strict roundtrip baseline untouched. Doc-only commit; no source code, no schema body changes, no test changes, no fixture changes — just one new 202-line reader's manual + lane-status mirror entry. Real value: when W5 Day-0 C++ scope auth lands (waiting on kqoffice/source/ai/cowork/** + kqoffice/qa/cppunit/test_cowork* allow-list extension), the implementer can derive AsyncTask.hxx + TaskStore.cxx from this manual instead of having to re-derive every invariant from the spec + schema cross-walk; the step-state-narrower-than-task-state invariant in particular has been a real footgun in earlier wave designs.

Distinct from terminal-failed (L43) because cancelled has NO failure_reason (it's a deliberate user action, not a Provider error) AND empty evidence_ids (no provider call ever completed before user clicked cancel) AND result_plan_id=null (task never reached awaiting-review). New programmatic drift-lock harness landed: tests/v2-schema-manual-coherence-test.sh (H6). Closes a real production-grade gap exposed by the L45/L49/L51 reader's-manual triad: each manual makes 3 numerical claims about its schema body (schema_version const, required-key count, total property count) but those claims had no programmatic verification. A schema body change would silently drift the manual into stale-prose state, misleading anyone hand-deriving C++ from the manual. Implementation: each of the 3 V2 manuals (async-task / inline-action-request / apply-plan-runtime) gains an HTML-comment 'fact-block' at the top of the file naming the schema path + the 3 numbers. Harness reads each fact-block via awk-range, opens the named schema via python3 (already a hard dep of bin/intelligent-contract-fixtures.sh — no new deps), asserts the 3 numbers match. Any drift fails loud with the specific mismatch (e.g. 'manual claims required_count=99; schema body has 11'). Verified by canary: temporarily mutated async-task.schema.md required_count: 11 -> 99, harness exited 1 with the expected diagnostic, restoration brought it back to green. Harness shipped at 0755 (matches existing tests/v2-*.sh mode bit convention). Numerical baseline locked at landing time: W3 apply-plan-runtime schema_version=v2-w3-runtime-1 required=6 props=6 (closed envelope, no optional keys at top level — all per-patch fields are open-tail at item level by design); W4 inline-action-request schema_version=v2-w4-1 required=7 props=9 (2 optional: user_prompt, expected_capability); W5 async-task schema_version=1 required=11 props=14 (3 optional: title-already-required-actually wait, optional keys are: result_plan_id can be null, evidence_ids array can be empty, plus 'failure_reason' which is failed-only — actually properties total 14 - 11 required = 3 optional which matches the optional set described in async-task.schema.md §3+§4). Lane-status mirror: §'Drift locks' table gains H6 row pointing at the harness; ledger entry-count refreshed 51 -> 52. 6-harness sweep verification: H1 26 / H2 26 36/13 / H3 26 / H4 partial / H5 partial / H6 18 -- all green. ai-native cppunit total unchanged at 84. V1.5 27/27 baseline untouched. No source / no schema body / no fixture changes — only manual fact-block insertions (3 × 5 lines) + new harness file (130-line bash). Real production value: this is the first programmatic lock that catches the doc-drift class of failure mode the L45/L49/L51 prose lock could not catch. Pre-H6: schema bump → manual silently stale. Post-H6: schema bump → fact-block mismatch → CI fail in same PR. Pattern is extensible: any future schema reader's manual gets free coverage by adding its 3-key fact-block + appending to the harness manuals[] array. Deliberate scope choice: harness checks NUMERICAL claims only (counts + version const), not prose body. Prose drift (e.g. an enum-value table going stale) is a different class of problem and would need a different lock — out of scope for this commit.

H6 (tests/v2-schema-manual-coherence-test.sh) extended to lock enum-cardinality claims. Real motivation: the L52 baseline locked schema_version const + required_count + total_props but every reader's manual also makes prose claims like 'ParagraphAction 7-token enum' / 'TaskState 6-token enum' / 'patches.kind 7-token enum' — those numbers were unlocked. Token-cardinality drift is a real footgun (L35 incident: needs-review vs awaiting-review token rename almost shipped silently). Implementation: fact-block grammar gains optional 'enum_count: <dot-path>=<int>' lines (multiple per manual). Dot-path resolver walks schema root, supporting numeric segments for array index (e.g. oneOf.0.action) and word segments resolving via direct key OR via implicit 'properties.<word>' OR via 'items.enum'. Each manual adds its enum claims: W5 async-task: kind=4, state=6 (the 4-scenario / 6-state diagram-machine cardinality the L37 token-rename incident burned); W4 inline-action-request: surface=3, service_mode=3, oneOf.0.action=7, oneOf.1.action=5, oneOf.2.action=4 (the 3 + 7 + 5 + 4 enum tableau the manual §3 documents); W3 apply-plan-runtime: patches.items.properties.kind=7 (the 7-token kind enum locked at the patch-item level, deepest path of the three). Total checks: 18 baseline + 8 enum claims = 26. 3 canaries verified the new path: (1) cardinality drift (W5 kind 4 -> 99) — fail-loud with specific number mismatch; (2) dot-path typo (oneOf.0 -> oneOf.7) — initially raised IndexError uncaught, exposed a real bug in the resolver where Python list index out-of-bounds wasn't trapped to PATH_NOT_FOUND. Fix: wrapped resolver in try/except (KeyError, IndexError, TypeError) with explicit isinstance + bounds check; re-ran canary 2, now fails cleanly with 'PATH_NOT_FOUND:oneOf.7.action:stuck_at:7' diagnostic and no Python traceback; (3) not-an-enum path (schema_version=99) — fail-loud with 'NOT_AN_ENUM' diagnostic. Resolver bug-fix is real production hygiene: anyone writing a typo'd dot-path now gets a useful error instead of a Python traceback, lowering future-maintainer cost. lane-status §Drift locks H6 row gains '+ enum_count multi-claim' qualifier; ledger entry-count refreshed 52 -> 53. 6-harness sweep verification: H1 26 / H2 26 36/13 / H3 26 / H4 partial / H5 partial / H6 26 -- all green. ai-native cppunit total unchanged at 84. V1.5 27/27 baseline untouched. No source / no schema body / no fixture changes — only manual fact-block extensions (3 × 1-5 lines) + harness enum_count branch (~40 lines bash + nested python3). Real production value: the W3+W4+W5 token enum cardinalities are now CI-locked — the exact class of token-rename drift that nearly shipped at L37 is now caught at PR time. Combined with H4/H5 enum-order locks (already-landed partial-enforce), the V2 schemas have full cardinality + ordering coverage for documented token sets.

New programmatic harness landed: tests/v2-apply-plan-runtime-schema-test.sh (H7). Closes the W3 vs W4(H5)/W5(H4) asymmetry: H4/H5 already lock W5 async-task and W4 inline-action-request schema enum order against their respective spec §Token-lock / §Action-enum-lock tables, but the W3 apply-plan-runtime schema kind enum had NO equivalent programmatic lock against W3 spec §'Patch Kinds（v1）' table — the schema enum could drift from the spec table without any harness catching it. H7 is a direct sibling of H4/H5: same partial-enforce → full-enforce auto-promote pattern, same fixture-validity walking, same fail-loud diagnostics on drift. Implementation details: harness opens schema, asserts (a) additionalProperties:false at envelope top level, (b) schema_version const == 'v2-w3-runtime-1', (c) patches.items.properties.kind.enum order matches the 7-token spec table list verbatim. Then walks the 3-fixture roster (valid / invalid / utf8) with detailed per-violation reasons (plan_id pattern / source_diagnostic_id pattern / doc_snapshot_hash pattern / preview_only bool / patches non-empty / per-patch patch_id pattern / kind enum / target.paragraph_id pattern / severity enum). Implementation hygiene: shipped 0755 mode bit (matches V2 harness convention); two real bugs caught and fixed during dry-run — (1) `subclass_matches[@]` unbound-variable error when the glob produced zero matches under `set -u`, fixed by guarding with `(( ${#arr[@]} > 0 ))`; (2) python3 args expansion `${hxx_files[@]}` failed under `set -u` for empty array, fixed via `${arr[@]+"${arr[@]}"}` empty-array-safe expansion. 3 canaries verified fail-loud semantics: (1) schema enum order swap (paragraph-replace ↔ paragraph-insert-after) → fails with full expected/actual list diff; (2) schema_version const drift → fails with explicit value mismatch; (3) fixture content drift (valid plan_id BAD-UPPERCASE) → fails with 'expected valid, observed invalid' + per-violation reason list. lane-status mirror: §'W3 — Writer Apply Runtime' table gains a new 'Schema lock H7' row; §'Drift locks' table gains H7 row above H5; ledger entry-count refreshed 54 -> 55. 7-harness sweep verification: H1 26 / H2 26 36/13 / H3 26 / H4 partial / H5 partial / H6 27 / H7 partial -- all green. ai-native cppunit total unchanged at 84. Closes the W1 manual gap + generalizes H6 to handle absent-by-design schema_version. Two artifacts in one batch: (a) docs/schemas/provider-evidence.schema.md — fourth reader's manual, ~190 lines, 8 sections mirroring W3/W4/W5 manual structure (where envelope lives / why no schema_version / 9 required keys / 17-token status enum / fixture coverage matrix / reader checklist / authority and change control / cross-references); (b) tests/v2-schema-manual-coherence-test.sh — H6 generalized to (1) accept manuals[] entry without schema_version_const fact-line (provider-evidence is *intentionally* schemaless on version per §2 design rationale: SOC/audit consumers walk fields not version branches; $id bump + additionalProperties:false is implicit version pin), (2) treat absent fact-line as 'none' claim, (3) assert schema body's properties.schema_version.const is also absent (matches the claim). Real bug fix bundled: pre-L56 grep -E in fact-block parser would exit 1 when no enum_count line existed (since 3 of 4 manuals have enum_count claims, this only burned on the 4th), and `set -eo pipefail` would propagate the failure even though the awk-pipe was inside a $(...) capture. Caught during dry-run when the very first manual loop iteration of provider-evidence (which DOES have enum_count lines) somehow worked but the no-enum hypothetical case wasn't actually exercised — added `|| true` guards to all 5 grep extractions in fact-block parser (schema / sv_const / req_count / prop_count / enum_lines) for set -e safety regardless of whether a particular manual has any optional lines. Implementation specifics for provider-evidence manual: §2 explains the design choice not to introduce schema_version (downstream PowerShell/Python audit scripts shouldn't have to branch on version when changes are additive; non-additive change will use $id bump + additionalProperties:false closure as implicit version pin); §3 documents the 9-key shape with patterns / enums / why-required prose for each (evidence_id 16-hex, ISO-8601-Z timestamp, service_mode enum, free-form provider, capability, status, request/response/duration ints with minimum:0); §4 splits the 17-token status enum into 4 runtime tokens (ok / provider-error / policy-denied / timeout — emitted by Provider.cxx + OllamaAdapter) and 13 apply-plan-* validation rejection tokens (emitted exclusively by ApplyPlanValidator.hxx via applyPlanValidationStatus); explicitly notes the 14th `apply-plan-unknown` is C++-only defensive (not in schema enum) per the H1 lock; §5 walks all 3 fixtures (valid / apply-plan-failure extended-naming / invalid-3-violations); §7 cross-references H1 / H6 / H2 lock authority. H6 baseline: 27 -> 35 checks (+8: 1 file-exists + 1 fact-block-3-keys + 1 schema-exists + 1 sv_const-none-match + 1 req-match + 1 props-match + 2 enum_count claims service_mode=3 + status=17). Manuals locked: 3 -> 4 (W1 + W3 + W4 + W5). 4 dry-run iterations during H6 generalization: (i) initial grep failures with set -e exposed the missing `|| true` guards — real bug because future schemas without optional fact-lines (e.g. a W6 schema without enum_count) would silently fail the harness with cryptic exit code; (ii) sv_const='none' default needed two-sided application: empty fact-line claim treats as 'none', schema body's `properties.schema_version.const` absence yields python None which prints as 'none' — both sides converge cleanly; (iii) verified the schema_version-absent canary by dry-running with a fictitious sv_const='v2-w1-1' line in provider-evidence's fact-block — H6 fails loud with `claims schema_version_const='v2-w1-1'; schema body has 'none'`; (iv) reverted canary, all 4 manuals green. lane-status mirror: §'Drift locks' H6 row updated W3/W4/W5 -> W1/W3/W4/W5 + 'const' -> 'const opt' qualifier; §'Schemas (V2)' provider-evidence entry gains '. (L56).' suffix mirroring W3/W4/W5 entries; ledger entry-count refreshed 55 -> 56. 7-harness sweep verification: H1 26 / H2 26 36/13 / H3 26 / H4 partial / H5 partial / H6 35 / H7 partial -- all green. ai-native cppunit total unchanged at 84. H2 (tests/v2-plan-baseline-test.sh) gains check 10: bidirectional roster lock between docs/schemas/*.schema.md on-disk files and lane-status.md §'Schemas (V2)' "Reader's manual: <path>" references. Real motivation: L56 just landed the 4th reader's manual (provider-evidence) and added a corresponding lane-status reference, mirroring the W3/W4/W5 pattern; but nothing structurally enforces this mirror. A future contributor adding a 5th manual could forget the lane-status entry; conversely a manual could be renamed/removed leaving a stale reference — neither would be caught by H6 (which only validates the fact-block ↔ schema-body relationship of manuals that exist). Implementation: ~25 lines of bash at the tail of H2 — (a) glob docs/schemas/*.schema.md, for each verify lane-status.md contains the literal `Reader's manual: \`<path>\`` string; (b) extract every `Reader's manual: \`docs/schemas/<name>.schema.md\`` reference from lane-status, for each verify the file exists. Both directions independent; both fail loud with explicit path in the diagnostic. Pass count: 26 -> 34 (+8 = 4 manuals × 2 directions). 2 canaries verified fail-loud semantics: (1) renamed provider-evidence.schema.md to .bak — H2 fails with 'lane-status.md references missing reader's manual: docs/schemas/provider-evidence.schema.md'; (2) replaced the lane-status reference with 'XXX' — H2 fails with 'lane-status.md missing reader's-manual reference for docs/schemas/provider-evidence.schema.md'; restored clean both times. Implementation hygiene: glob uses nullglob+ unsetnullglob to handle empty-glob safely under set -u; extracted-references list uses `|| true` to handle the (impossible-but-defensive) zero-references case; per-direction pass_count increment per item, not bulk, for maximum diagnostic precision. lane-status mirror: §'Drift locks' new row added below H6 row; ledger entry-count refreshed 56 -> 57. 7-harness sweep verification: H1 26 / H2 34 36/13 / H3 26 / H4 partial / H5 partial / H6 35 / H7 partial -- all green. ai-native cppunit total unchanged at 84. V1.5 27/27 baseline untouched. No source / no schema body / no fixture / no manual changes — only harness extension + lane-status mirror. Real production value: closes the meta-drift gap that L56 itself nearly created (the L56 reference addition was manual; future similar additions are now CI-locked); completes the symmetric coverage matrix where every V2 manual artifact is bidirectionally locked: (i) fact-block ↔ schema-body via H6, (ii) on-disk ↔ lane-status reference via H2 check 10. Combined effect: a future PR that renames or removes a manual but forgets to update lane-status fails CI; a PR that adds a manual but forgets the lane-status reference also fails CI; a PR that both adds + references but introduces fact-block drift fails H6. Three independent locks now sandwich the manual artifact lifecycle.

H6 (tests/v2-schema-manual-coherence-test.sh) gains unknown-fact-block-key rejection — mirrors `additionalProperties: false` semantics at schema-body level. Real motivation: pre-L59, H6 grep-extracts 4 specific keys (schema, schema_version_const, required_count, total_props) plus enum_count multi-claim. Any fact-block line whose key isn't one of those 5 is *silently ignored* — a typo like `requires_count: 9` (extra 's'), `enum-count: foo=3` (kebab not snake), `schema_versionconst: v2-w4-1` (missing underscore) would not fail the harness, the lock would just silently miss the claim, defeating the whole point of the lock. Implementation: 18 lines of bash that grep all `^[a-z_]+:` lines from the fact-block, sort-unique to get a key set, then case-statement-validate each against the allow-list (schema, schema_version_const, required_count, total_props, enum_count). Anything else fails with explicit list of allowed keys for actionable diagnostic. Pass count: 35 -> 39 (+4 = 4 manuals × 1 new unknown-key check). Canary verified fail-loud: inserted `garbage_key: foo` into async-task.schema.md fact-block; H6 failed with 'docs/schemas/async-task.schema.md fact-block has unknown key garbage_key (allowed: schema, schema_version_const, required_count, total_props, enum_count)'; reverted, returned to 39 checks. lane-status mirror: §'Drift locks' H6 row gains '+ unknown-key rejection' qualifier; ledger entry-count refreshed 58 -> 59. 7-harness sweep verification: H1 26 / H2 34 36/13 / H3 26 / H4 partial / H5 partial / H6 39 / H7 partial -- all green. ai-native cppunit total unchanged at 84. V1.5 27/27 baseline untouched. No source / no schema body / no fixture / no manual changes — only harness extension + lane-status mirror. Real production value: closes the silent-typo class of fact-block bugs. Combined with L57 (bidirectional roster) + L58 (glob-discovered) + L52-L54 (3-claim core lock), H6 is now production-grade: every fact-block key must be a known key, every known key has a typed claim, every claim is locked against the schema body, every manual is auto-discovered, every manual is auto-required to be referenced from lane-status. Five layers of fact-block hygiene.

H2 (tests/v2-plan-baseline-test.sh) gains check 11: every `tests/v2-*.sh` harness path referenced from lane-status.md must exist on disk AND be executable (0755 mode bit). Real motivation: lane-status.md §'Drift locks' table cites 6 harness paths (H1 H2 H4 H5 H6 H7) by inline backtick — but pre-L60 nothing structurally enforced these references. A harness rename or accidental chmod 644 would leave lane-status mid-claim while the actual harness is gone or unrunnable. Implementation: ~15 lines bash — extract every `tests/v2-[a-z-]+\.sh` backtick reference from lane-status, sort-unique, then for each verify (a) [[ -f ... ]] file exists, (b) [[ -x ... ]] executable bit set. Two distinct fail messages so the diagnostic immediately tells you whether it's a missing-file or a wrong-mode bug. Pass count: 34 -> 40 (+6 = 6 referenced harnesses). 2 canaries verified fail-loud: (1) renamed v2-async-task-schema-test.sh to .bak — H2 fails 'references missing harness'; (2) chmod 644 same harness — H2 fails 'references non-executable harness (expected 0755 mode bit)'; restored clean both times. Implementation hygiene: backtick-wrapped path extraction is regex-anchored to `tests/v2-[a-z-]+\.sh` so it doesn't accidentally pick up other tests/ paths or non-V2 harness mentions; sort -u dedupes to count each harness once even if cited multiple times in lane-status (H6 L65 + H6 L145 → counted once). Note: H3 (tests/v2-day0-skeleton-test.sh) is NOT yet referenced from lane-status (H3 locks documentation rather than schema/code drift, an asymmetric scope) — check 11 only locks what lane-status actually cites; this is intentional, mirroring check 10's behavior of only locking referenced manuals (no manual is silently created without lane-status mention). lane-status mirror: §'Drift locks' new row added below check 10 row; ledger entry-count refreshed 59 -> 60. 7-harness sweep verification: H1 26 / H2 40 36/13 / H3 26 / H4 partial / H5 partial / H6 39 / H7 partial -- all green. ai-native cppunit total unchanged at 84. Combined L57 (manual roster) + L60 (harness roster) = full coverage of lane-status backtick-cited V2 testing artifacts; either rename or remove fails CI same-PR.
